full_text:
Professor Shuangge Ma

Editor-in-chief

Journal of Briefings in Bioinformatics

Dear Editor,

On behalf of all authors, I am pleased to submit our manuscript entitled
"***Tribus: Semi-automated discovery of cell identities and phenotypes
from multiplexed imaging and proteomic data***" to *Briefings in
Bioinformatics* for consideration the possibility of its publication.

[Multiplexed imaging and proteomic techniques allow researchers to
quantify and monitor cellular activity at single-cell resolution levels.
However, cell-type annotation has become a critical and challenging task
due to e.g. complex marker combinations, batch effects and artifacts,
and the labor-intensive nature of manual gating.]{.mark}

[In this manuscript, we present **Tribus**, a knowledge-based classifier
designed to perform cell-type annotation in multiplexed imaging and
proteomic datasets. Tribus uses a logic table as prior knowledge for the
classification, and can solve the challenges by:]{.mark}

- [Allowing flexible adjustment of markers and target cell
 > types.]{.mark}

- [Applying the logic table per sample iteratively.]{.mark}

- [Overcoming the need for labor-intensive setting of thresholds per
 > marker channel.]{.mark}

We exhaustively benchmark Tribus with similar methods on four public
datasets with ground-truth labels. Tribus outperformed the other methods

holds per
 > marker channel.]{.mark}

We exhaustively benchmark Tribus with similar methods on four public
datasets with ground-truth labels. Tribus outperformed the other methods
in terms of efficiency and accuracy. Importantly, Tribus can recover
fine-grained cell types as accurately as human experts while allowing
the discovery of phenotypically new subtypes. We then showcase the
usability of Tribus on two large in-house whole slide image datasets. To
ensure smooth integration of Tribus with different image-analysis
workflows, we have developed a user-friendly Napari plug-in for Tribus
enabling fast visualization and interpretation of the results.

We believe that our manuscript will be of great interest to the readers
of *Briefings in Bioinformatics*, as it reports a novel tool to solve
the critical needs in the analysis of large-scale multiplexed imaging
and proteomic data.

We confirm that the data in this manuscript has not been submitted to,
nor published in any other primary scientific journal and that all
authors are aware of, and agree with, the manuscript\'s contents.

We greatly appreciate your time in consideration of our work.

Sincerely yours,

Anniina Färkkilä, MD PhD

Assistant Professor in Translational Gynecologic Oncology

Email: anniina.farkkila@helsinki.fi

Research Program in Systems Oncology & Department of Obstetrics and
Gynecology
